title: scleranthus perennis subsp. polycnemoides
intro: Scleranthus perennis subsp. polycnemoides, also known as annual knawel, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Scleranthus perennis subsp. polycnemoides include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine.
